items.title: Two production Next.js apps, built solo with Cursor+Claude, hit $13,945
items.why_it_matters: A solo developer shared their exact workflow for shipping two profitable Next.js apps using Claude, proving that your current stack is more than enough to build real, revenue-generating software. They break down how to manage context windows and avoid getting stuck in endless refactoring loops. This week, read their architectural breakdown and apply their 'one-file-at-a-time' prompting strategy to your AI-ingest repository.

items.title: Cc-fleet: Run cheaper LLMs as Claude Code workers
items.why_it_matters: This tool lets you route cheaper models like DeepSeek or Llama as background workers inside Claude Code while keeping Claude as the main driver. This directly addresses your goal of saving API costs during heavy, repetitive coding sessions without losing Claude's reasoning quality. This week, install cc-fleet and run your next major Next.js refactoring task using DeepSeek to see how much you save on token costs.

items.title: Save: An API that turns any URL into clean Markdown for LLMs
items.why_it_matters: A simple API that converts raw web pages into clean, LLM-ready Markdown. Since you are actively building AI-ingest to filter your news, scraping and cleaning raw HTML is a bottleneck you don't need to write from scratch. This week, plug this API into your AI-ingest backend to instantly clean up incoming article links before feeding them to Claude.

items.title: Pulse: Dashboard for Claude Code to approve tool calls from your phone
items.why_it_matters: Pulse is a self-hosted dashboard that lets you monitor Claude Code terminal tasks and approve tool executions directly from your mobile browser. This means you don't have to sit at your desk waiting for long-running generation or testing loops to finish. This week, spin up Pulse on your local machine and try approving a Supabase schema migration while away from your keyboard.

items.title: LoopFlow: Loop engineering for Claude Code
items.why_it_matters: LoopFlow automates repetitive write-test-fix cycles inside Claude Code, keeping the AI focused on resolving errors systematically rather than hallucinating new code paths. It helps prevent the tool from getting stuck in infinite loops when debugging. This week, run LoopFlow on your AI-ingest repository to automatically detect and patch any broken TypeScript types in your Next.js API routes.
